title: Level 594.1
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Area- 51 }} {{CSS|content=@import url('https://fonts.cdnfonts.com/css/bruno-ace'); /* Font */ html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Bruno Ace', sans-serif; } }}
{{Blurquote|quote-text={{Alt Class|no=594.1|ent=5|ext=3|env=5}}|image=Facilityhallway.png}}
{{Transparent Theme 2}} {{Blurbackground|file=Area52.webp}}
==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Area52 2.png|caption=Photo taken of Level 594.1}} Level 594.1, also known as "Area-51", consists of highly restricted military facility resembling a U.S. base of the same name. The level consists of long, sterile corridors, vast underground complexes, rooms with their own respective purpose, and aircraft hangars-many of which are locked storing defunct experimental technology. Additionally, the level is dim, with few working lights illuminating the whole level. Not all wanderers seen in the level are actual, tangible beings. Any wanderers that appear "fuzzy" are merely hallucinations. Conventional navigation and communication tools such as compasses, maps, and walkie-talkies do not function. More advanced devices, such as smartphones, remain partially functional, though many applications behave erratically. A rare phenomenon involving the intercom system presents a further hazard. At unpredictable intervals, the speakers activate and begin addressing a wanderer directly. The voice often recalls traumatic events, unresolved failures, or personal insecurities, eventually mocking the listener. During the event, loud whispers echo the intercom’s words in repetition, growing increasingly distorted before stopping without warning. Documented outcomes include severe psychological trauma consistent with the progression of Roth Syndrome, or, in less extreme cases, permanent tinnitus caused by the auditory intensity. This phenomenon is exceedingly uncommon, with only five recorded instances.
== Extra Areas == The facility contains a range of specialized rooms, each serving a distinct function. Dormitories are lined with rows of bunks and lockers, occasionally containing uniforms or personal belongings that disappear on later visits. Containment cells vary in scale, with some designed for humanoid figures and others large enough for massive unidentified organisms. Research laboratories contain outdated computers, specimen containers, and surgical equipment, though most documentation recovered is heavily redacted or destroyed. Weapon storage rooms, often marked with stenciled labels such as “Weaponry,” contain a rotating selection of firearms and prototypes, differing each time they are accessed. Sewer and utility tunnels run beneath the complex, carrying stagnant water that poses a severe biohazard risk. Large meeting rooms with long tables and bolted-down chairs are also present; faint voices are sometimes heard within them, as though meetings continue in absence of participants. === Sewers === {{Blurimage|file=Sewers51.jpg|caption=Sewers.}} Sewers can be accessed by finding a ladder descending into the ground. The sewers of Level 594.1 contain entities such as [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], and the [[Hydrolitis Plague]]. The sewage water contains a more mutated version of the Hydrolitis Plauge, being able to make people sick just by a small contact of the water. It is highly discouraged to enter the sewers for any reason. === Weapon Room === {{Blurimage|file=Weaponry.jpg|caption=Weaponry.}} Weapon rooms can be discovered by exploring the level until you come across a room marked "Weaponry." These rooms contain a variety of firearms, including the AK-47, R870 shotgun, Kalthoff Rifle, AN-94, Kriss Vector, and more. Each Weapon Room offers a different selection of weapons. Every 4 hours, the already taken guns will be found again on its original location. Aditionally, Base Blackspire left resources for wanderers to assist their survival. === Laboratories === Labs are frequently used by the bases that reside in the level. Labs are the most guarded area of the Level, as they hold top secret experiments, and formulas. Human corpses are a recurring feature within these rooms. Bodies decompose at an accelerated rate, often reaching skeletal stages within hours, or disappear entirely without trace. Doors within the level frequently exhibit autonomous behavior, locking or unlocking at random intervals and occasionally trapping wanderers inside.
== Entities == The environment appears to function as a containment site for both familiar and undocumented entities. Numerous holding cells show signs of deterioration, and reports confirm that some entities roam outside their designated containment. Wretches are observed in increased numbers, and even Dullers have been encountered within unsecured areas. The level also supports anomalous microorganisms, including the Hydrolitis Plague, as well as several recognizable strains of Frontrooms bacteria and viruses. === Foreigner's Foe === [[File:Monoculi.jpg|center|thumb|354x354px]]Foreigner's Foe, known scientifically as ''Monoculex'' (''Monoculectes'' in plural), are grotesque, skinny, one-eyed aberrations that are aggressive to all lifeform except for its own and a few other entities. ''Monoculectes'' are carbon copies of each other, with very little of them having unique anatomy from each other. ''Monoculectes'' are mostly made of bone and skin, with a little margin of flesh hiding at their shoulders and thighs. Foreigner's Foe are typically 8ft tall and very skinny as if they are malnourished, even though they are, in a sense. Monoculectes has a very fast metabolism, and are in a constant state of starvation, which explains how they can eat absolutely anything they desire. ''Monoculectes'' also has supernatural vision, being able to see very far. ''Monoculectes'' do not have hands, as they are replaced with sharp, elongated bone like spears designed for stabbing prey or other lifeforms. To top it off, ''Monoculectes'' can run for 5 miles per hour without getting tired most of the time. Infant ''Monoculectes'', or ''Monoculectes'' in general are very intelligent entities, with infant ''Monoculectes'' having the intelligence similar to that of a crow, but ''Monoculectes'' are also more intelligent the older they are. ''Monoculectes'' will always cooperate with each other if they have the chance. ''Monoculectes'' have very unique and gruesome ways of reproducing. Female ''Monoculectes'' will have small, but many bubbles on their back, presumably eggs. The older they get, the bigger the bubbles become, until they pop, and release infant ''Monoculectes'', and also killing the mother. Destroying the bubbles before they pop will also kill the female ''Monoculectes'' and the infants. Do note, that this only works for female ''Monoculectes''. === Thanamorphs === [[File:Thanamorphs3.png|center|thumb|315x315px]] Thanamorphs, known scientifically as ''Cadaverivora ferox'', are believed to be mutated variants of Wretches, sharing the same behavior as one. Thanamorphs often exhibit extreme physical alterations, such as weapons forcibly embedded into their bodies, or massive crab-like pincers protruding from their backs. Many have been found wearing pieces of armor. Thanamorphs are completely blind but has an incredibly heightened sense of hearing, capable of hearing faint noises in a 30 mile radius. They do not require food for survival; instead, they slowly consume their own biomass over time, which limits their lifespan to roughly five months before dying. Despite their grotesque looks, Thanamorphs are mindless, behaving much like brainless zombies. They are easily fooled and can often be outrun by prepared wanderers. But their low intelligence is offset by their defense mechanisms, which vary depending on the mutations present. These may include sharp, bone like thorns protruding through their skin, corrosive acid spit, or bone-like armor defending a part of their body. Thanamorphs, while durable, are highly vulnerable to intense heat, as their necrotic flesh burns rapidly and cannot regenerate. Severe damage to their central nervous cluster located deep within the chest cavity—will incapacitate or kill them instantly, regardless of remaining biomass. Loud, sustained noises can disorient them, temporarily hindering their ability to track prey. === Glare === [[File:Glare.png|center|thumb|338x338px]]The Glare, known scientifically as ''Oculivorax insania'', is an anomalous, seemingly two dimensional entity resembling a static, flat image of multiple human like eyes arranged around a massive central pupil. Its form does not cast shadows, reflect light, or display any physical depth appearing almost like a projection. Despite its immobile, "picture-like" appearance, the Glare will always seem to be facing its observer, with every eye unnervingly tracking movement in perfect unison. The entity does not make movements and stays in place, it simply exists in one’s vision, occasionally vanishing and reappearing closer without any transition. Looking into the Glare's eyes is an mental hazard. Staring at it for more than a few seconds gives your eyes a very strong stinging pain. The longer you look, the worse it gets, such as paranoia, seizures, vision blurring, and ultimately irreversible insanity. Prolonged fixation results in the victim’s mind deteriorating to the point where their body undergoes Phenomenon 35. Strangely, the Glare does not consume, touch, or interact physically with anything. Witnesses often claim that after an encounter, its eyes continue to appear in reflections, shadows, and even the backs of their eyelids, as though the image has permanently burned itself into their vision. To kill a Glare, one must reflect a Glare's own image, killing the Glare slowly as it looks at itself. Anyone can do this by using mirrors, or phone cameras (with the risk of it experiencing software failure.)
== Bases and Outposts == There are currently 3 bases ran by [[Entity Research Committee]] and [[Level 594]]'s residence === Base Blackspire === Base Blackspire is operated by a group originating from Level 594, mostly part of New America. Our job is exterminating most of the entities in the level, and experimenting with them. We welcome lost wanderers and lead them out of danger. === [[Entity Research Committee|ERC]] Base Aleph-9 === Base Aleph-9 mostly assists Base Blackspire with their duty. But we also try to find new, undocumented entities and report them to the database to get them checked up. === Rapid Response Team of Nevada === Led by the people of Nevada, they act as backup for Base Blackspire if anything goes wrong. RRTON is also the first base set up on Level 594.1, with its founder being Dean Miller. This base has a consistent number of members, with there being 130 of us.
== Entrances & Exits == === Entrances === *Going to Alaska and entering Area-51 on [[Level 594]]. *Finding a metal door labelled Area-51 and entering it. === Exits === *Find an emergency exit door and running through the long hallway will bring one back to [[Level 594]] or [[level 15]]. *Entering an aircraft hanger and going into one of their jets will bring one to [[Level 36]]
